news.Title: Broadcom CEO unnerves biggest AI backers in rattling pivot
news.Content: Broadcom ( AVGO ) just posted the kind of quarter most chipmakers can only dream about. Record revenue, AI sales up triple digits, and a growing roster of marquee customers. Then the stock fell. Shares of Broadcom dropped 12.59% on June 4 to close at $418.91, the steepest one-day fall in over a year. The slide didn’t stop there. By the close on June 9, the stock sat at $396.60, down 18.64% across five trading days. The stock’s price drop may be tied to something CEO ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom reported record revenue and triple-digit growth in AI sales, but its stock price dropped significantly.
• The company reaffirmed its AI semiconductor revenue target of over $100 billion by fiscal 2027, rather than increasing it, unsettling investors.
• Broadcom announced a pivot to selling 'chips only,' stepping back from integrated AI systems, which investors expected to offer higher margins.
• AI semiconductor revenue reached $10.8 billion last quarter, a 143% year-over-year increase, contributing to total revenue of $22.19 billion.
• Google, Broadcom's largest AI customer, is expected to diversify its chip suppliers, posing a risk of market share loss for Broadcom.
news.Keywords: AI chips, revenue growth, gross margin, customer diversification, integrated systems

news.Title: The AI Trade Just Had Its Worst Stretch in Months. Here Are 3 Reasons the Sell-Off Could Be a Buying Opportunity.
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) trade just went through its roughest stretch in months. After a nine-week run higher, the major indexes pulled back last week, and the selling peaked on Friday, when the Nasdaq Composite fell about 4.2% -- its steepest single-day drop since early 2025. Chip stocks took the worst of it, with the broad semiconductor group sliding about 10% in that single session. The sell-off appeared to be tied to several things. Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) posted record results ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The AI trade experienced a significant sell-off, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping 4.2% and semiconductor stocks falling about 10% in a single session.
• Factors contributing to the decline include Broadcom's results not raising AI chip outlook, higher bond yields from a strong jobs report, and Middle East tensions.
• Despite the pullback, underlying AI spending remains strong, with major cloud operators planning ~$700 billion in capex for data centers in 2026.
• Broadcom reported record Q2 revenue of $22.2 billion (up 48% YoY), with AI chip revenue up 143% to $10.8 billion, and expects AI chip sales to grow over 200% this quarter.
• Nvidia reported record Q1 FY27 revenue of $81.6 billion (up 85% YoY), with its data center business growing 92%.
news.Keywords: AI trade, semiconductor sell-off, data center capex, revenue growth, valuation reset

news.Title: Best Buy bets big on controversial partner

Now, not only can you buy these wearable computers, but you can actually try them on at a familiar technology retailer: Best Buy. news.Title: Tesla's China Sales Just Snapped a 2-Month Slump With a 22% Jump. Is the Turnaround Real?
news.Content: China retail sales of electric vehicles made by Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) reached 47,281 units in May, up 22.5% year over year, according to data released Monday by the China Passenger Car Association. The figure snapped a two-month run of year-over-year declines and marked the electric-car maker's first year-over-year increase in the world's largest auto market since February. It was also a steep jump from a weak April, with retail volume rising about 82% from the prior month, when Tesla 's China sales had sagged to fewer than 26,000 units. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's China retail sales in May increased by 22.5% year-over-year to 47,281 units, snapping a two-month slump.
• This marks the first year-over-year increase in China since February, with a significant 82% jump from April's sales.
• The rebound was led by the Model Y (up 39% YoY wholesale) and Model 3 (up 41% YoY wholesale), despite an overall market decline in new energy vehicles (-7.5% YoY).
• A refreshed lineup and aggressive financing offers, including zero-interest plans and insurance subsidies, likely drove the May sales increase.
• However, year-to-date sales are still down nearly 8%, and the sustainability of the rebound depends on genuine demand versus promotional pull, especially as competition intensifies.
news.Keywords: China sales, EV market, demand recovery, financing incentives, competitive landscape

news.Title: Intel Stock Has Risen Sixfold from Its 2025 Lows, but Don’t Expect a Dividend Yet
news.Content: As I have said multiple times, the term "corporate turnaround" is much abused. However, Intel (INTC) could be a textbook example of a turnaround stock. INTC is up almost sixfold from its 2025 lows, and what was once dead money turned into a FOMO stock. Intel’s turnaround has been in the works for quite some time now. In 2021, company veteran Pat Gelsinger took over as the CEO and embarked upon “transforming” Intel under the IDM 2.0 strategy. The strategy focused on making Intel a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel's stock has risen significantly (sixfold from 2025 lows), positioning it as a potential corporate turnaround story under CEO Pat Gelsinger's IDM 2.0 strategy.
• The strategy involves becoming a foundry player and focusing on AI chips, though initial foundry customer acquisition and AI chip revenue (Gaudi) fell short of expectations.
• Intel received crucial backing from the U.S. government (10% stake), SoftBank, and Nvidia, providing necessary capital.
• Key foundry wins include Microsoft as a customer and a preliminary agreement with Apple; Intel is also joining Elon Musk's 'Terafab' project for Tesla and SpaceX.
• Alphabet (Google) has reportedly ordered over 3 million TPUs from Intel, and Nvidia is testing Intel's 18A process, validating its foundry capabilities amidst onshoring trends.
news.Keywords: corporate turnaround, foundry strategy, AI chips, government backing, onshoring

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Intel Becomes Plan B for the Semiconductor Industry. Here's How That's a Winning Strategy
news.Content: The semiconductor industry has spent the last several years confronting a reality few expected: there simply aren't enough advanced chip manufacturing options. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) has become the dominant producer of leading-edge chips, powering everything from artificial intelligence accelerators to smartphones. As AI demand continues to expand, customers are increasingly looking for alternatives—not necessarily to replace TSMC, but to ensure they have a backup plan if capacity becomes constrained. That possibility ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) is the dominant producer of leading-edge chips, but customers are seeking alternatives due to capacity concerns.
• Intel (INTC) is emerging as a 'Plan B', with reports of Google placing an order for over three million TPUs starting in 2028.
• Intel shares rose 11% on the Google news, contributing to a YTD gain of 204% and a 12-month gain of 448%.
• Nvidia (NVDA) is also reportedly evaluating Intel as a potential manufacturing partner, building on a previous test of Intel's 18A process and a $5 billion investment.
• Intel's strategy focuses on becoming a trusted second source for manufacturing, rather than directly competing with TSM's market share.
news.Keywords: semiconductor manufacturing, AI chips, foundry, capacity constraints, diversification

• 執行長潘健成指出，AI已進入推論時代，邊緣AI裝置產生龐大資料量，驗證了AI推論以資料與儲存為核心的趨勢。
• 他強調，AI推論應用普及帶動NAND儲存需求強勁，市場供需結構將持續緊俏，沒有降溫跡象。
news.Keywords: 營收創高,AI推論,NAND Flash,供需緊俏,歷史新高
